task_completion.description: Open NASA Panoply and load the NCEP Air Temperature dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply application window is open, and 'air.mon.ltm.nc' is visible in the Sources or Datasets panel.

task_completion.description: Create a 1D time-series line plot for the land coordinate (45N, 260E).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 1D line plot window in Panoply showing temperature ('air') over time. The plot or its settings panel should indicate the coordinates are set to Latitude 45 (or 45N) and Longitude 260 (or 260E / 100W).

task_completion.description: Create a 1D time-series line plot for the ocean coordinate (45N, 190E).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 1D line plot window in Panoply showing temperature ('air') over time. The plot or its settings panel should indicate the coordinates are set to Latitude 45 (or 45N) and Longitude 190 (or 190E / 170W).

task_completion.description: Create a 2D spatial map of global air temperatures for the month of August.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D spatial map window in Panoply showing global air temperatures. The time dimension setting (usually at the top or side of the plot window) must be set to August (e.g., index 8 or 'Aug').

task_completion.description: Export the three required image files to the output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager window, terminal, or save dialog showing 'land_annual_cycle.png', 'ocean_annual_cycle.png', and 'august_global_map.png' saved in the '~/Documents/ThermalLecture/' directory.

task_completion.description: Create a structured findings report containing the extracted peak temperature months and values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open showing the contents of 'thermal_inertia_report.txt'. The text must identify July as the peak month for land (~22.77 °C) and August as the peak month for ocean (~15.50 °C), saved in '~/Documents/ThermalLecture/'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Panoply GUI to generate the plots and maps.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the Panoply graphical interface (clicking menus, adjusting plot settings, using export dialogs) rather than running a Python script to generate the images.

integrity.description: The agent visually extracted the peak temperatures from the Panoply plots or data arrays.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen viewing the 1D plots or the Array tab in Panoply to find the peak values before writing them into the report, rather than typing them from memory or a hidden script.

integrity.description: The agent manually created the text report using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: A GUI text editor (like gedit, mousepad) or terminal editor (nano, vim) should be visible with the agent typing or pasting the findings, rather than a script instantly generating the text file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Dataset: '/home/ga/PanoplyData/air.mon.ltm.nc'. Variable: 'air'. Land target (45N, 260E) peaks in July at ~22.77 °C. Ocean target (45N, 190E) peaks in August at ~15.50 °C. Output directory: '/home/ga/Documents/ThermalLecture/'. Required files: 'land_annual_cycle.png', 'ocean_annual_cycle.png', 'august_global_map.png', 'thermal_inertia_report.txt'.
